target.name: Matic Robotics
target.founder: Navneet Dalal, Mehul Nariyawala, Shafaq Presswala

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $7M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Matic Robotics is a seed-stage consumer technology company founded by Navneet Dalal, Mehul Nariyawala, and Shafaq Presswala. The company has raised $107M to date. No comparable public or private companies were available to inform this valuation.

Given the absence of comparable company data, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology. Using total capital raised of $107M as a proxy baseline, and applying a typical seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x, the analysis implies a valuation of $18M. This reflects the expectation that value creation occurs between the current seed stage and subsequent financing rounds.

The triangulated valuation range is $15M to $20M, with a median of $18M. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 2.5x multiplier for this specific company and sector; the absence of peer comparables limits ability to validate stage-appropriate progression rates. Additionally, the relationship between total capital deployed ($107M) and current stage progression remains unclear without visibility into burn rate, deployment timeline, and capital allocation across milestones. Investors should require detailed financial forecasts and milestone-based justification before finalizing any valuation.
